title: Kids Tea Cart
summary: A kid-height rolling cart on repurposed stair spindles for legs, with a scalloped trim skirt and a carry handle on top.
description:
Stair spindles make surprisingly good turned legs for a small cart — they're inexpensive, already shaped, and just need trimming to length and drilling for casters. Measure the square section at the base of each spindle carefully before cutting the leg notches into the bottom shelf; that square section is what registers the spindle's position and keeps all four legs sitting at matching angles.

Glue the caster wheels into the drilled leg bottoms rather than just press-fitting them — a cart pushed around by a kid takes real lateral stress on those wheel joints, more than the casters' own hardware alone is built to resist.

tools.note: Cutting the leg notches and scalloped trim.

materials.name: Plywood strips, 2-1/2" wide, 8 ft
materials.note: Scalloped trim — 1x material or purchased trim works too.

cutList.note: Notched to fit the square base of each spindle.
Cut to fit around the cart's perimeter.

steps.title: Notch the bottom shelf and trim the spindles
steps.body: Measure the square base section of the stair spindles and cut a matching notch out of the bottom 1x12 with a jigsaw. Cut the spindles down to their finished leg length.

steps.title: Attach the legs to the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the trimmed spindles to the bottom 1x12 with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and wood glue applied to the underside.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top to the tops of the legs with 2" wood screws, predrilling every hole and using glue.

steps.title: Add the caster wheels
steps.body: Drill holes in the bottom of each leg and insert the caster wheels, gluing them in place for extra hold against the lateral stress of daily use.

steps.title: Build and attach the handle
steps.body: Build the handle from a 1x2 base and 1x3 sides, then attach the assembled handle unit to the top of the cart with 1-1/4" screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Add the scalloped trim and finish
steps.body: Cut the plywood or trim strip to fit around the cart's perimeter, scallop the lower edge with a jigsaw, and attach with glue. Sand everything smooth and finish.

images.alt: A small rolling kids tea cart with turned stair-spindle legs and a scalloped trim skirt
